block_03: Congratulations on officially becoming a Sabio Trader! Now you are free to trade with our funds on your live account.
block_04: Below you will find your account login details. The rules of trading on the live account are the same as with the assessment one — start trading right away!
block_10: You can make your first withdrawal anytime if your live account is in profit. To request a withdrawal, log in to your Dashboard and submit a request in the Profit Share section. The next withdrawal request can be placed not earlier than 30 days after the previous one.
block_11: Note: upon the first withdrawal request, the Maximum Trailing Drawdown will be locked on your starting balance.*
block_12: With any further questions, reach us at
block_15: We’re happy to have you onboard
block_16: *The Maximum Trailing Drawdown is the maximum amount of money you can lose. If you exceed this threshold, you will lose your funded account.
block_18: If your starting balance is $100,000, you can draw down to $94,000 before you violate the Maximum Trailing Drawdown rule.
block_19: If you increased your account to $103,000, this will be considered your new high-water mark. Thus, your new Maximum Trailing Drawdown would be $97,000.
block_20: Next, let's say your account is now $103,000 in closed balance, which would be your new high-water mark. At this point, your Maximum Trailing Drawdown would be locked in at your starting balance of $100,000. So, regardless of how high your account goes, you would only breach this rule if your account draws back down to $100,000 (note that you can still violate the Daily Loss Limit). For example, if you take your account to $170,000, as long as you do not draw down more than 3% in any given day, you would only breach if your account equity reaches $100,000.
block_21: Disclaimer: Trading carries a high level of risk, and may not be suitable for everyone. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Before getting involved in trading, you should carefully consider your personal objectives, level of education/experience, and risk appetite. You must be aware that the possibility exists that you could sustain a loss of some or all of your initial deposit and therefore, you should not trade with funds which you cannot afford to lose. The information contained in this email does not constitute financial advice in any form, nor is it a solicitation to buy or sell any Forex contract or securities of any type.
